name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in analytical characterization, stability/degradation analysis, and DOE-guided process development for organic, polymer, and thin-film systems. Applies MS/GC-MS/LC-MS exposure, NMR/GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA/DSC, and technical writing to build reproducible analytical control strategies and CMC-ready documentation.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided bio-based polymer process development, converting material-quality data into reproducible process windows and scale-up specifications.
Designed and characterized organic molecular materials, mapping structure, purity, and stability across 15+ variants with spectroscopy, NMR/GPC, thermal analysis, electrochemistry, and MS.
Drove AFOSR-funded thin-film coating process development, linking CVD/surface preparation to morphology, interface quality, and optical performance.
Optimized CVD polymer thin-film deposition with DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring to define growth kinetics and reproducible process controls.
Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented calibration, SOP, and safety systems that improved instrument uptime by 30%.

projects.title: Analytical Characterization & Stability
projects.description: Connected molecular structure, purity, and degradation behavior using orthogonal spectroscopy, electrochemistry, and thermal methods.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, CV, TGA/DSC, NMR, GPC, MS

projects.title: AFOSR Thin-Film Process Development
projects.description: Established process-property relationships for CVD/surface-preparation workflows and reproducible conditions.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface prep, thin films

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Scale-Up Controls
projects.description: Defined DOE-guided process windows and material-quality specifications for reproducible polymer scale-up.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Analytical Characterization: LC-MS/GC-MS exposure, NMR, GPC/SEC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, cyclic voltammetry, mass-spectrometry-informed analysis
skills.Process & CMC Documentation: DOE, SPC, method documentation, analytical control strategy mindset, reproducibility studies, SOPs, technical reports, CMO/CTL transfer adjacency
skills.Materials & Stability: Organic/small-molecule materials, polymers, thin films, solid-state/thermal analysis, degradation/failure analysis, impurity-control mindset
skills.Data & Communication: Cross-functional presentations, root-cause analysis, Excel, Python/basic data analysis, JMP/basic, careful technical writing

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
